programs.name: Kentucky Medicaid
programs.what_it_does: Provides health coverage for low-income seniors, including those who are aged or disabled. Covers medical expenses for eligible elderly individuals age 65 and above.[4][6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+, disabled individuals, and other qualifying low-income Kentuckians based on income and household size.
programs.benefit_value: Coverage for medical expenses; lasts up to 12 months with annual recertification.

programs.name: Kentucky Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) Waivers
programs.what_it_does: Offers services to allow seniors and disabled individuals to receive care at home instead of in institutions. Includes personal care, homemaker services, and other supports to promote independence.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors and disabled individuals eligible for nursing facility level of care but preferring home-based services.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by waiver program and individual needs assessment.

programs.name: Kentucky PACE
programs.what_it_does: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly provides comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors. Enables participants to live safely in the community with capitated Medicare and Medicaid funding.
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home eligible seniors aged 55+ who can live safely in the community with PACE services.
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of approved medical and long-term care services.

programs.name: Kentucky Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Helps low-income Medicare beneficiaries pay for Medicare Part A and B premiums, deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments. QMB covers full premiums and cost-sharing; SLMB and QI cover premiums only.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors on Medicare with income and resource limits specific to each category.
programs.benefit_value: Varies: QMB up to $1,255 Part B premium + cost-sharing; SLMB/QI premium assistance.

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits to purchase food for eligible low-income households. Seniors 60+ have simplified application processes and medical expense deductions.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ and other eligible households; income limit $1,580/month for individual.

programs.name: Kentucky Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides financial assistance to help low-income households pay heating and cooling bills. Application period runs October through March.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors and other households meeting income guidelines.

programs.name: Kentucky Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Improves energy efficiency in homes of low-income households through insulation, weatherstripping, and other upgrades. Reduces energy costs and improves home safety.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors and households, priority often given to elderly and disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Free home energy improvements valued at thousands of dollars.

programs.name: Kentucky State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free counseling and assistance on Medicare options, including Part D, Medicare Advantage, and supplemental coverage. Helps access prescription assistance through KPAP partnership.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors, disabled individuals, families, and caregivers in all 120 Kentucky counties.
programs.benefit_value: Free personalized counseling; prescription drug savings.

programs.name: Kentucky Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Often coordinates with Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ who are unable to prepare their own meals.
programs.benefit_value: Meals delivered 5 days/week; suggested donation for those who can contribute.

programs.name: National Family Caregiver Support Program / Kentucky Family Caregiver Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides support services including respite care, caregiver training, and counseling. Offers flexible benefits to family caregivers of seniors.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Informal caregivers of individuals 60+, Alzheimer's patients, or relatives 55+ caring for children.
programs.benefit_value: Respite care, training, counseling; service hours vary by assessment.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time community service work and training for low-income seniors. Helps transition to unsubsidized employment.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work experience up to 20 hours/week.

programs.name: Kentucky Legal Aid for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance for income-eligible seniors on issues like benefits, housing, and consumer protection. Often through Area Agencies on Aging.

programs.name: Kentucky Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents of long-term care facilities to protect rights and resolve complaints. Provides education and oversight of nursing homes and assisted living.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and other long-term care facilities in Kentucky.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy services and complaint resolution.

programs.name: Home and Community Based (HCB) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: This Medicaid waiver program covers the cost of long-term care services for seniors either in a nursing home or at home. It includes the opportunity to pay family caregivers to assist with daily activities.
programs.who_its_for: Kentucky residents age 65 or older who meet Medicaid income and asset limits, require a nursing facility level of care, and have functional needs due to physical or cognitive impairments.
programs.whats_unique: Kentucky's specific implementation allows payment to caregivers under this state-administered waiver, supporting home-based care beyond standard federal Medicaid structure.
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of approved home care services for eligible individuals, subject to income/asset limits and potential waiting lists.

programs.name: Money Follows the Person
programs.what_it_does: This program assists seniors transitioning from nursing facilities back to home or community settings. It provides support for long-term care services in less restrictive environments.
programs.who_its_for: Kentucky seniors previously in nursing homes who meet Medicaid eligibility, including age 65+, income/asset limits, and nursing facility level of care needs.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded initiative tailored to Kentucky's Medicaid system to promote community reintegration, not universally available in all states.
programs.benefit_value: Covers transition services and ongoing home/community-based care costs for eligible participants.

programs.name: State-Funded Senior Meals Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides meals to seniors through senior centers and other providers. State funding restores and supports daily meal services that may face federal budget constraints.
programs.who_its_for: Kentucky seniors relying on community meal programs, particularly those facing service cuts.
programs.whats_unique: Direct state-funded allocation (e.g., $9 million boost) to maintain meals program independently of federal funding levels.
programs.benefit_value: $9 million in state funding to restore most cut services through June of the fiscal year.
